---
title: "Empty resolv.conf after boot but ppp link is active"
date: 2011-04-09
forum: Networking &amp; Wireless

### Post by localhorst42 on 2011-04-09
[INDENT]Hello,

As a new ubuntu user (version 10.10) I currently struggle with the network setup.  In the network manager GUI  I configured a PPP connection with "IPv4 Settings: Automatic (PPPoE)" and "Available to all users", "Connect automatically" both checked.

Most of the time (not always though) after boot my resolv.conf contains only the line

```
# Generated by NetworkManager
```Everything starts working again if I either move the resolv.conf from /etc/ppp to /etc (the ppp link is active!) or just unplug the cable and plug it in again.

Any advice how to debug this would be appreciated,

David
[/INDENT]

---

### Post by dineshs on 2011-04-10
> I configured a PPP connection with "IPv4 Settings: Automatic (PPPoE)" Instead, select  [COLOR="Blue"]Automatic(PPPoE)addresses only[/COLOR]  and enter DNS address in the respective field (if you have two DNS addresses seperate them using a comma eg:  [COLOR="Blue"]4.2.2.1,8.8.8.8[/COLOR] )
Note : The details are stored in */etc/NetworkManager/system-connections/DSL* assuming that your connection name is [COLOR="Blue"]DSL[/COLOR].The entry in /etc/resolv.conf is important when you configure your devices via /etc/network/interfaces.This is what I understand

---
